essence: Provides operational, logistical, and administrative support to police, fire, and ambulance teams during routine work and major incidents.

identity.title: Emergency Services Support Officer
identity.summary: Assists emergency teams with logistics, equipment management, scene support, documentation, and coordination during incidents and daily operations.

salary.notes: Specialists in major‑incident logistics, evidence support, or multi‑agency coordination can exceed £40k-£50k+.

qualifications: Emergency services support or incident‑response training
Knowledge of equipment handling and scene‑safety procedures
Ability to work under pressure and follow structured protocols

lifestyle.description: Emergency services support officers assist police, fire, and ambulance teams with logistics, equipment, scene management, and documentation. They may help set up cordons, transport equipment, support responders at incidents, and manage operational resources. The role suits individuals who enjoy varied work, teamwork, and contributing to public safety without being on the frontline.

habits: Prepares and checks equipment before shifts
Follows strict safety and incident‑scene procedures
Communicates clearly with responders and control rooms
Responds flexibly to changing incident needs
